mechanistic_claim: Selective binding and clearance of toxic protofibrils reduces amyloid-driven synaptic dysfunction; amyloid PET shows dose-dependent plaque reduction
notes: CLARITY-AD: 27% CDR-SB slowing; full approval 2023; magnitude near MCID debated

mechanistic_claim: Targets N-terminal pyroglutamate amyloid (N3pG) for rapid plaque clearance to amyloid-negative status; amyloid PET shows 84% reach negativity
notes: TRAILBLAZER-ALZ 2: 35% iADRS slowing in low/medium tau; FDA full approval; time-limited dosing concept

mechanistic_claim: High-dose anti-amyloid antibody clears aggregated amyloid plaques, slowing cognitive and functional decline in early AD
notes: EMERGE hit primary; ENGAGE null; accelerated approval 2021, withdrawn 2024; poster child for amyloid controversy

mechanistic_claim: Binding soluble amyloid-beta monomers shifts equilibrium away from plaque formation, preventing amyloid-driven neurodegeneration before clinical symptoms
notes: A4 prevention trial: HR 1.08 for progression; PACC null; no amyloid PET change; total monomer strategy failure

mechanistic_claim: Peripheral sink hypothesis: binding soluble Abeta in plasma draws brain amyloid into circulation for clearance, slowing cognitive decline
notes: EXPEDITION3: 11% non-sig CDR-SB slowing; primary ADAS-cog missed; peripheral sink hypothesis abandoned

mechanistic_claim: Inhibiting beta-secretase (BACE1) reduces amyloid-beta production at source, preventing plaque formation and downstream neurodegeneration
notes: EPOCH: stopped for futility; HR 1.05; prodromal trial also stopped; BACE class abandoned across industry

mechanistic_claim: Inhibiting gamma-secretase blocks final cleavage step in amyloid-beta production, reducing toxic peptide generation
notes: Stopped early: WORSE than placebo on cognition and function; Notch pathway off-target toxicity; harmful

mechanistic_claim: GLP-1 agonism reduces insulin resistance and neuroinflammation in AD brain; epidemiological association between T2D and AD suggests metabolic pathway to neurodegeneration
notes: EVOKE/EVOKE+ both null; no CDR-SB separation from placebo despite biomarker improvements

mechanistic_claim: Ginkgo extract EGb 761 reduces oxidative stress and improves cerebral blood flow, preventing age-related cognitive decline and dementia onset
notes: GEM trial: HR 1.12 (0.94-1.33); 3069 participants; 6 year follow-up; no benefit for dementia prevention

mechanistic_claim: Estrogen replacement protects against neurodegeneration via anti-inflammatory, neurotrophic, and cerebrovascular mechanisms; observational studies showed 29-44% dementia risk reduction
notes: WHIMS: HR 2.05 (1.21-3.48) — INCREASED dementia risk; direct contradiction of observational evidence

mechanistic_claim: B-cell depletion via anti-CD20 reduces CNS inflammation and meningeal B-cell follicles driving progressive neurodegeneration; EBV reservoir in B-cells may be upstream driver
notes: ORATORIO: HR 0.76 (0.59-0.98); first ever PPMS-approved DMT; EBV-B-cell mechanism well-established

mechanistic_claim: B-cell depletion via anti-CD20 reduces relapse-driving inflammation; superiority over interferon demonstrates B-cell centrality in MS pathogenesis
notes: OPERA I/II: HR 0.60 (0.45-0.81) vs IFN; head-to-head superiority; anti-CD20 now standard of care

mechanistic_claim: Blocking alpha-4 integrin prevents lymphocyte trafficking across blood-brain barrier, reducing CNS inflammation and relapse rate
notes: AFFIRM: 42% CDP reduction; initially withdrawn for PML risk, reintroduced with monitoring; mechanism well-validated

mechanistic_claim: Selective depletion of lymphocytes (especially B and T cells) via adenosine deaminase inhibition produces durable immune reconstitution, reducing MS relapse and disability progression
notes: CLARITY: HR 0.67 (0.48-0.93) for 3mo CDP; oral tablet; approved EU 2017, US 2019

mechanistic_claim: Functional antagonism of S1P1 receptors sequesters lymphocytes in lymph nodes, preventing their migration to CNS and reducing inflammatory relapses
notes: FREEDOMS/TRANSFORMS: 48% ARR reduction; first oral MS DMT; mechanism well-understood

mechanistic_claim: S1P-mediated lymphocyte sequestration reduces disability progression independently of relapse reduction, via direct neuroprotective effects on oligodendrocytes
notes: FREEDOMS II: HR 0.83 (0.61-1.12) p=0.227; NULL for CDP; relapse-disability dissociation

mechanistic_claim: Activating Nrf2 pathway upregulates antioxidant response elements and shifts immune balance from pro-inflammatory Th1/Th17 to anti-inflammatory Th2, reducing CNS inflammation
notes: DEFINE/CONFIRM: 44-53% ARR reduction; oral; prior-IFN subgroup rate ratio 0.55

mechanistic_claim: Statins provide neuroprotection in progressive MS via anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and endothelial effects independent of cholesterol lowering; pilot showed 43% brain atrophy reduction
notes: MS-STAT2: HR 1.01 (0.82-1.25); large definitive trial; null on all endpoints including sNfL; high-profile neuroprotection failure

mechanistic_claim: High-dose biotin enhances mitochondrial energy production and promotes myelin repair in progressive MS by activating acetyl-CoA carboxylase and fatty acid synthesis
notes: SPI2: OR 1.35 (0.81-2.26); all endpoints negative including sNfL; initial pilot had shown improvement

mechanistic_claim: MR shows genetically-lowered vitamin D increases MS risk (OR 2.0); supplementation should reduce disease activity by restoring immunomodulatory vitamin D signaling
notes: VitD meta OR 0.66 for relapse proportion but VIDAMS null HR 1.17; D-Lay HR 0.66 for composite but relapse alone null
